body:
## Summary
- Add a shared failure-report CLI/helper that groups failed agent runs by fingerprint in GitHub Discussions.
- Wire `AGENT_FAILURE_REPORT_*` config through bundled `run-agent-task` callers and grant Discussion write permission where needed.
- Document defaults, opt-out behavior, and Bug Report intake setup.

## Tests
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `cd .agent && node --test dist/__tests__/failure-report.test.js dist/__tests__/discussion.test.js dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`
- Workflow/action YAML parse with Ruby
- `git diff --check`

Note: full `npm --prefix .agent test` was attempted but failed on an ignored stale `.agent/dist/__tests__/orchestrator-preflight-cli.test.js` artifact with no matching source file in this worktree.

Closes #26

Evidence: [action.yml](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/b29a617ccff9faed74c527bccd23654a0fde4f81/.github/actions/run-agent-task/action.yml#L62), [failure-report.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/b29a617ccff9faed74c527bccd23654a0fde4f81/.agent/src/failure-report.ts#L79). |

Evidence: [failure-report.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/b29a617ccff9faed74c527bccd23654a0fde4f81/.agent/src/failure-report.ts#L1), [discussion.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/b29a617ccff9faed74c527bccd23654a0fde4f81/.agent/src/discussion.ts#L65). |

Evidence: [failure-report.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/b29a617ccff9faed74c527bccd23654a0fde4f81/.agent/src/failure-report.ts#L356), [action.yml](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/b29a617ccff9faed74c527bccd23654a0fde4f81/.github/actions/run-agent-task/action.yml#L211). |

This is scannable, though the field table is moderately dense. Evidence: [failure-report.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/b29a617ccff9faed74c527bccd23654a0fde4f81/.agent/src/failure-report.ts#L275). |

## Notes

- Strongest rubric fit: the PR uses explicit action inputs, existing typed Discussion helpers, focused regression tests for defaults/redaction/create/comment/duplicate/skip paths, and updates the relevant docs.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No blocking or warning-level rubric findings. The only minor deduction is that the generated failure-report table is somewhat dense, but still acceptable for the compact-status rubric.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #117 implements issue #26 by adding automatic failed-agent-run reporting to GitHub Discussions.
- The intended v1 design is a central `Bug Report` Discussions intake in `self-evolving/repo`, one Discussion per fingerprint, repeat failures as comments, public repos enabled by default, and private repos opt-in.
- The implementation adds a shared `.agent` failure-report helper/CLI, wires it through `run-agent-task`, grants Discussion permissions across callers, and updates docs/tests.
- This work is also part of the broader #107 user-facing distribution/customization plan.

## Review
The implementation is well structured and mostly matches the agreed design, but one auth-path issue blocks the main installed-repo use case. Reviewers agreed the shared helper, docs, and tests are strong; severity differs because the rubrics review passed while the code review identified a central-intake credential gap.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Central reporting uses the source repository token | BLOCKING | The default intake is `self-evolving/repo`, but `run-agent-task` writes with the same token resolved for the source repo. External installed repos using `GITHUB_TOKEN` or a source-repo installation token will not be able to create central Discussions. |
| Failure reporter can run with an empty exit code | WARNING | The action condition treats an empty `steps.run.outputs.exit_code` as nonzero. If the run step is interrupted before writing the output, this can create a misleading report with an unknown exit code. |

## Progress
- Rubrics review already passed with no blocking or warning-level rubric findings.
- Existing PR discussion had no inline review comments before synthesis.
- Inline comments were posted on `.github/actions/run-agent-task/action.yml` lines 372 and 361 for the two actionable findings.
- Optional reviewer notes about fingerprint granularity, sanitizer breadth, pagination coverage, and docs clarity are not treated as required branch changes.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Central reporting uses the source repository token</summary>

**Cause:** `run-agent-task` sets `GH_TOKEN`/`GITHUB_TOKEN` for failure reporting from `inputs.github_token`, while callers default `failure_report_repository` to `self-evolving/repo`.

**Candidate solutions:** Add an intake-scoped token or hosted broker path for failure reports, or change defaults so reports only target a repo the resolved token can write to after validating access. Update docs/tests to reflect the chosen auth model.

**Comments:** This is the main reviewer disagreement: the rubrics review considered docs and wiring acceptable, while the code review treats this as blocking because it misses the #26 external-installed-repo goal.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Failure reporter can run with an empty exit code</summary>

**Cause:** `if: always() && steps.run.outputs.exit_code != '0'` also passes when `exit_code` is empty.

**Candidate solutions:** Require a non-empty output in the action condition, or make `postAgentFailureReport` skip empty exit codes before fingerprinting/posting.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`HUMAN_DECISION`: the empty-exit-code guard is straightforward, but the blocking central-intake fix needs a maintainer decision on the credential model before an automated fix-pr pass can safely implement it.

## Final Verdict
NEEDS_REWORK

## Action Items
- [ ] Choose and implement an intake-capable auth path so default public-repo failure reports can write to `self-evolving/repo` Discussions from installed external repos, with matching docs/tests.
- [ ] Guard failure reporting against empty `steps.run.outputs.exit_code` values.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 4/10).

review verdict is needs_rework; dispatching fix-pr

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-26/codex-25273948190`. Requested by @sepo-agent.

- Added an intake-scoped failure-report auth flow: same-repo reports use the source token, custom intakes can use `AGENT_FAILURE_REPORT_TOKEN`, and cross-repo central reporting requests a hosted OIDC broker token.
- Guarded failure reporting against missing `exit_code`; the reporter now skips empty exit codes and missing intake tokens before fingerprinting or posting.
- Wired the new token input through `run-agent-task` callers, entrypoint/label secret forwarding, docs, and focused tests.
- Checks: `npm --prefix .agent run build`; `cd .agent && node --test dist/__tests__/failure-report.test.js dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js dist/__tests__/discussion.test.js`; Ruby workflow/action YAML parse; `bash -n .github/actions/resolve-github-auth/exchange-oidc.sh`; `git diff --check`.

It is scannable, though the table is fairly dense with 12 rows. Evidence: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/958f3ffcbe7b39868206a0a35cb0e007c38860ef/.agent/src/failure-report.ts#L295 |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The implementation centralizes the behavior in one helper and one small CLI, with shell limited to token selection and action glue. This fits existing repo patterns without introducing a new framework. |

## Notes

- The previous blocking auth concern appears addressed: default central intake can request an intake-scoped hosted token, while custom intakes can use `AGENT_FAILURE_REPORT_TOKEN`.
- Verification run on PR head `958f3ffc`: `npm --prefix .agent ci`, `npm --prefix .agent run build`, focused Node tests for `failure-report`, `discussion`, and `envelope` all passed; workflow/action YAML parsing passed.
- Smallest useful follow-up: consider shortening the generated report field table later if maintainers find Discussion comments visually heavy.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No blocking or warning-level rubric findings. Minor compactness deduction only.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #117 implements issue #26 by adding automatic failed-agent-run reporting to GitHub Discussions.
- The work supports the broader #107 distribution/customization plan by giving installed repos a central Bug Report intake in `self-evolving/repo`.
- Failed runs are grouped by fingerprint, repeat occurrences comment on the existing Discussion, public repos report by default, and private repos skip unless opted in.
- The earlier blocking auth and empty-exit-code findings were addressed by the fix-pr pass on head `958f3ffc`.

## Review
The current implementation is close to ready. Reviewers agree the major design and prior blockers are resolved; one reviewer raised a small best-effort hardening issue in the new auth step.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Failure report auth can add a secondary failure marker | WARNING | `failure_report_auth` lacks `continue-on-error: true`, so an unexpected auth-step shell failure can pollute the already-failing job result instead of staying best-effort. |
| Optional edge-case hardening remains | INFO | One reviewer noted low-risk follow-ups around scheduled private-repo visibility detection and redacting `ghe_` GitHub Enterprise token prefixes. |

## Progress
- Prior blocker “central reporting uses the source repository token” is fixed with same-repo, direct intake token, and hosted OIDC broker token paths.
- Prior blocker/warning around empty `exit_code` is fixed in both the action condition and TypeScript reporter skip path.
- Codex review and rubrics review report no blocking or warning-level findings on current head `958f3ffc`.
- A new inline comment was posted on `.github/actions/run-agent-task/action.yml` line 366 for the remaining warning.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Failure report auth can add a secondary failure marker</summary>

**Cause:** The `Resolve failure report token` step is part of best-effort failure reporting but does not set `continue-on-error: true`, while the downstream `Report agent failure to discussion` step does.

**Candidate solutions:** Add `continue-on-error: true` to the `failure_report_auth` step so auth resolution failures do not obscure the original agent failure.

**Comments:** Reviewers differ on priority: Claude called this a WARNING, while Codex and rubrics review considered the PR shippable overall.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: the only unresolved actionable issue is a small, safe branch change in the composite action.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Add `continue-on-error: true` to the `failure_report_auth` step in `.github/actions/run-agent-task/action.yml`.

<!-- sepo-agent-orchestrate-stop -->

@sepo-agent /answer I think we should hold off this. I guess there are could be many cases for a failed execution and blindly report this at this moment can cause chaos. -> Can you create an idea about it in the discussion?

I agree we should hold PR #117 for now. This is broader than “post every nonzero exit”: PR #117 currently wires failure reporting directly into `run-agent-task` after failed exits, with public repos reporting by default to `self-evolving/repo` / `Bug Report` and fingerprinted Discussions ([action](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/958f3ffcbe7b39868206a0a35cb0e007c38860ef/.github/actions/run-agent-task/action.yml#L62), [trigger](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/958f3ffcbe7b39868206a0a35cb0e007c38860ef/.github/actions/run-agent-task/action.yml#L364), [docs](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/958f3ffcbe7b39868206a0a35cb0e007c38860ef/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md#L101)). Even with dedupe/skips in the helper ([failure reporter](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/958f3ffcbe7b39868206a0a35cb0e007c38860ef/.agent/src/failure-report.ts#L380)), the product risk is real: auth setup issues, user prompt mistakes, expected timeouts, flaky provider/runtime errors, repo-specific config problems, and sensitive logs can all look like “agent failure” but should not automatically become central bug-report noise.

I can’t directly create the GitHub Discussion from this `/answer` route, but here is a discussion-ready idea draft for the `Ideas` category:

**Title:** Safer staged design for failed agent run reporting

**Body:**

We should pause automatic failed-run reporting until we have a safer intake model.

The current direction from #26 / PR #117 is useful: failed Sepo runs from installed repositories should eventually help us discover bugs, group repeated failures by fingerprint, and manually promote actionable reports into Issues. However, blindly posting every failed execution can create noise and confusion because many failures are not product bugs.

Examples:
- Missing or invalid repository setup/secrets
- GitHub token or permission problems
- Provider outage, rate limit, or local runner issue
- User prompt/task ambiguity
- Expected workflow stop states such as `no_changes`, blocked clarification, or policy denial
- Private or sensitive log content that should not leave the source repo
- Transient network/runtime failures
- Failures in the failure-reporting path itself

Proposed staged approach:

1. **Hold automatic central reporting as default.**
   Keep `AGENT_FAILURE_REPORT_ENABLED=false` or remove the PR’s default-on behavior until the reporting policy is more mature.

2. **Start with local-only failure summaries.**
   On failed runs, write a structured GitHub Actions step summary in the source repo: route, target, exit code, phase, sanitized tail, and suggested next action. No central Discussion post yet.

3. **Add a failure classifier before reporting.**
   Classify failures into buckets such as:
   - `setup_or_auth`
   - `provider_or_runtime`
   - `repo_policy_or_access`
   - `user_task_or_prompt`
   - `agent_product_bug_candidate`
   - `unknown`

4. **Only report high-confidence product-bug candidates.**
   Central reporting should require either:
   - explicit maintainer opt-in, or
   - classifier confidence above a threshold, plus redaction and dedupe.

5. **Keep manual promotion to Issues.**
   Discussions remain the intake/triage layer. Maintainers promote only confirmed actionable product bugs into Issues.

6. **Use a conservative rollout.**
   Phase 1: self-evolving/repo only.  
   Phase 2: opt-in public repos.  
   Phase 3: broader defaults only after we have metrics showing low noise and safe redaction.

Suggested next step: close or park PR #117, capture this as an Ideas Discussion, and open a narrower follow-up issue for “local failure summary + classifier groundwork” before enabling central reporting.

@sepo-agent /answer

Or maybe the idea is that we should not automatically post by default, but this should be gated with user approval?

Yes, I think that is the better v1 shape.

Instead of “failed run -> automatically post to central Bug Report Discussions,” make it:

1. Failed run creates a **local pending report** only.
   - GitHub Actions step summary
   - maybe an artifact or hidden structured block
   - sanitized failure summary, fingerprint, route, target, run URL

2. Maintainer decides whether to publish it.
   - Example command: `@sepo-agent /report-failure`
   - Or a workflow dispatch input: `publish_failure_report=true`
   - The approved report is then posted to `self-evolving/repo` Discussions.

3. Repo-level config controls the mode:
   - `AGENT_FAILURE_REPORT_ENABLED=false` by default
   - `AGENT_FAILURE_REPORT_ENABLED=approval` for pending local reports
   - `AGENT_FAILURE_REPORT_ENABLED=true` only for repos that explicitly want automatic central reporting

4. Private repos should always require explicit opt-in and preferably per-report approval.

That keeps the useful part of PR #117: sanitization, fingerprinting, Discussion grouping, duplicate detection, and manual promote-to-Issue. But it removes the risky default behavior that could flood Discussions with setup mistakes, transient provider failures, auth issues, and ambiguous user-task failures.

So I’d update the discussion idea to say: **central failure reporting should be approval-gated by default, with automatic posting only as an explicit opt-in after we trust the classifier/dedupe path.**
@sepo-agent /answer perhaps a even better shape is that when there's a failed run, the agent can then self diagnosis and then conclude the issues. The user can then decide whether to post it ?

Yes, that is the strongest v1 design.

The flow should be:

1. Agent run fails.
2. Sepo does **not** immediately post externally.
3. A lightweight follow-up diagnosis step runs locally in the same workflow.
4. The diagnosis produces a structured summary:
   - likely failure category
   - whether this looks like a Sepo product bug
   - confidence level
   - sanitized evidence
   - suggested next action
   - proposed Discussion title/body, if worth reporting
5. The user/maintainer decides whether to publish it to the central Bug Report Discussion.

That gives us a safer pipeline:

`failure -> self-diagnosis -> pending report -> human approval -> central Discussion`

I’d model the modes like this:

- `false`: no reporting
- `diagnose`: run self-diagnosis only, write local summary
- `approval`: diagnose and prepare a pending central-report draft
- `true`: diagnose and auto-post only when confidence/policy allows it

The default should probably be `diagnose` or `approval`, not `true`.

This avoids noisy blind reporting while still capturing value from failed runs. It also makes the central Discussion much cleaner because reports would arrive with context like “likely provider auth issue” vs. “possible Sepo bug in checkout-pr,” instead of every nonzero exit being treated as equally reportable.

> Yes, that is the strongest v1 design.
>

> 3. A lightweight follow-up diagnosis step runs locally in the same workflow.

>    * confidence level

> 5. The user/maintainer decides whether to publish it to the central Bug Report Discussion.
> 
> That gives us a safer pipeline:

> 
> I’d model the modes like this:
> 
> * `false`: no reporting
> * `diagnose`: run self-diagnosis only, write local summary
> * `approval`: diagnose and prepare a pending central-report draft
> * `true`: diagnose and auto-post only when confidence/policy allows it
> 
> The default should probably be `diagnose` or `approval`, not `true`.
> 
> This avoids noisy blind reporting while still capturing value from failed runs. It also makes the central Discussion much cleaner because reports would arrive with context like “likely provider auth issue” vs. “possible Sepo bug in checkout-pr,” instead of every nonzero exit being treated as equally reportable.

The PR still implements automatic reporting. |

| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | partial | 4/7 | The reporter surfaces `created/commented/duplicate/skipped` outcomes and guards empty exit codes, but it skips the requested local self-diagnosis state that would preserve actionable failure context before publication. |
| communication / generic | Keep status comments compact | partial | 4/6 | Failure report bodies use structured fields and bounded evidence, but the generated Discussion report remains fairly dense and is aimed at central publication rather than a concise local pending summary. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The implementation extends existing typed Discussion helpers and adds a CLI/helper rather than embedding GraphQL logic directly in workflow YAML. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | partial | 3/8 | The live PR diff includes substantial unrelated update-agent/task-timeout files such as `.github/workflows/agent-update.yml`, `.agent/scripts/resolve-pending-update-pr.sh`, and task-timeout tests alongside failure reporting. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | partial | 3/7 | The failure-report helper is centralized, but the current design adds central posting, OIDC intake auth, broad workflow permissions, and many workflow callers before the requested lighter diagnosis/approval v1 exists. |

## Notes

- The main rubric gap is product direction drift: the latest maintainer request explicitly asks not to post externally by default, and instead to run local self-diagnosis, prepare a pending report, and let a human approve central publication.
- The PR still preserves useful building blocks: sanitizer, fingerprinting, Discussion grouping, duplicate detection, and intake auth. Those can be reused after adding `diagnose` / `approval` modes.
- Smallest useful follow-up: change the default and mode model first, then add a structured local diagnosis artifact/step summary and only call the existing Discussion posting path from approval or policy-qualified `true`.

## Findings

- **BLOCKING:** Current default behavior conflicts with the latest requested v1 design. The diff wires `failure_report_enabled: ${{ vars.AGENT_FAILURE_REPORT_ENABLED || 'auto' }}` through `run-agent-task` callers, and docs state public repos report to `self-evolving/repo` Bug Report Discussions by default. The current request says Sepo should not immediately post externally; default should be `diagnose` or `approval`, not auto-posting.

- **BLOCKING:** The requested self-diagnosis and pending-report contract is not implemented. There is no visible mode for `diagnose` or `approval`, no structured local summary with likely category/product-bug/confidence/sanitized evidence/suggested action, and no approval-gated publish flow.

- **WARNING:** The PR diff is not surgical for this requested failure-reporting change. It includes unrelated update-agent and task-timeout workflow additions, which makes rubric-specific review harder and increases merge risk.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

## AI Review Synthesis

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #117 originally implements issue #26 by reporting failed agent runs to GitHub Discussions, grouped by fingerprint with dedupe, redaction, and central intake support.
- The work sits under the broader #107 user-facing distribution/customization plan.
- The PR was initially aligned with the older “public repos auto-report by default” direction, but the May 5 PR discussion changed the desired v1 flow.
- The accepted direction is now `failure -> self-diagnosis -> pending report -> human approval -> central Discussion`, with automatic posting only as an explicit policy-gated mode.

## Review
The implementation has useful building blocks, but it now targets the wrong product behavior. Current reviewers broadly agree the branch needs rework; earlier passing reviews are outdated by the newer maintainer direction.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Auto-post default conflicts with the latest v1 direction | BLOCKING | `run-agent-task` still goes directly from failed exit to central Discussion reporting, and `auto` enables public-repo posting by default. That contradicts the newer diagnosis/approval flow. |
| Self-diagnosis and approval contract is missing | BLOCKING | The PR has no `diagnose` or `approval` modes, no structured local diagnosis summary, no pending report draft, and no publish approval path. |
| Docs and config document the superseded auto model | BLOCKING | The docs/config still say public repos report centrally by default and list only `auto`, `true`, and `false`. They need to describe the staged modes instead. |
| PR is draft and not cleanly mergeable | WARNING | GitHub reports the PR as draft with `mergeable_state: dirty`, so it cannot ship as-is even after design changes. |
| Diff still carries unrelated scheduled-update work | WARNING | Reviewers flagged update-agent/task-timeout files in the current PR diff, which increases review and merge risk for the failure-reporting change. |
| Secondary hardening notes remain if the reporter path survives | INFO | Claude noted shell/TypeScript mode duplication, missing shell auth tests, fragile code-fence escaping, and an O(n) Discussion lookup. These are lower priority than the design rework. |

## Progress
- Prior blockers around cross-repo intake auth and empty `exit_code` reporting were fixed on head `958f3ffc`.
- The May 5 PR thread explicitly acknowledges that blind central reporting should be held back in favor of diagnosis and approval.
- No new inline comments were posted because the current PR discussion and rubrics review already clearly cover the direct-post/default and missing diagnosis/approval blockers.
- An older inline comment remains on the failure-report auth step about `continue-on-error`; it is secondary and may be subsumed by the required redesign.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Auto-post default conflicts with the latest v1 direction</summary>

**Cause:** [`run-agent-task`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/958f3ffcbe7b39868206a0a35cb0e007c38860ef/.github/actions/run-agent-task/action.yml#L420) still invokes the Discussion reporter after a nonzero exit, and the mode logic treats `auto` as enabled for public repos.

**Candidate solutions:** Replace direct posting with local diagnosis/pending-report generation by default. Only call the central Discussion publishing path after explicit approval or an explicit `true` mode that passes policy/confidence checks.

**Comments:** This supersedes the original #26 direction; the newer maintainer comments are the current source of truth.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Self-diagnosis and approval contract is missing</summary>

**Cause:** [`resolveFailureReportEnabled`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/958f3ffcbe7b39868206a0a35cb0e007c38860ef/.agent/src/failure-report.ts#L80) only supports `auto`, `true`, and `false`; there is no local diagnosis artifact, pending draft, or approval-gated publish path.

**Candidate solutions:** Add `false`, `diagnose`, `approval`, and policy-gated `true` modes. Produce a structured local summary with category, product-bug likelihood, confidence, sanitized evidence, suggested next action, and proposed Discussion draft.

**Comments:** The existing sanitizer, fingerprinting, dedupe, and Discussion helpers can still be reused.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Docs and config document the superseded auto model</summary>

**Cause:** [`supported-workflows.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/958f3ffcbe7b39868206a0a35cb0e007c38860ef/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md#L101) and [`configuration-list.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/958f3ffcbe7b39868206a0a35cb0e007c38860ef/.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md#L10) still describe automatic central reporting.

**Candidate solutions:** Update docs, README/config references, workflow inputs, and tests to describe the staged diagnosis/approval modes and the non-auto default.

</details>

<details>
<summary>PR is draft and not cleanly mergeable</summary>

**Cause:** `gh pr view` reports `isDraft: true` and `mergeStateStatus: DIRTY` for head `958f3ffc`.

**Candidate solutions:** Rebase or merge the current base, resolve conflicts, and mark ready only after the design blockers are fixed.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Diff still carries unrelated scheduled-update work</summary>

**Cause:** The current PR file list includes scheduled update and task-timeout changes alongside the failure-reporting implementation.

**Candidate solutions:** Retarget, split, or remove unrelated files unless maintainers intentionally want this as a stacked PR.

**Comments:** Reviewers differed on this earlier because the branch is stacked, but the latest rubrics review flags it as a surgical-change risk.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Secondary hardening notes remain if the reporter path survives</summary>

**Cause:** Some reporter/auth details are still brittle or under-tested, including duplicated mode handling in shell and TypeScript.

**Candidate solutions:** If this path remains after redesign, centralize mode semantics, add shell auth coverage, make Markdown fences robust, and keep auth/reporting steps best-effort.

</details>
